1. For each SAFE-STATIC old->new: 'git mv old new' then fix EVERY importer (grep the module path, update 'from X import' / 'import X' to the new path). After each rename run '/home/polaris/pipeline-env/bin/python -m pytest tests/ --collect-only -q | tail -1' — must stay at pytest tests/ --collect-only -q = 16738 tests collected, 11 errors (matches expected 16738/11). Worktree /home/polaris/wt/phase-file on branch chore/review-readiness-filerename, base commit c5448ec (chore/review-readiness-phase1). Oracle harness force-copied from phase0 (tests/oracle/*.py + 3 cassettes) and left UNSTAGED (git status: M cassette.py, M llm_cassette.py, ?? acceptance_portable.py, ?? retrieval_cassette.py, ?? cassettes/) — never committed. Note: the run_full_scale consolidation goal (Plan V4: collapse v10..v30 near-duplicate launchers into ONE parameterized run_full_scale.py with a --profile/--config flag) applies to the 8 SAFE-STATIC vNN scripts; v30_phase2 is held back because it is a live by-path dependency.; if it errors, the rename broke an importer you missed → fix or 'git mv' back + skip+document.
2. LAUNCHER CONSOLIDATION: if the run_full_scale_v10..v30 scripts are genuine near-duplicates differing only by a version/param, create ONE parameterized launcher (e.g. scripts/run_full_scale.py --variant vNN) that reproduces each script's behaviour via a param, and replace the duplicates with thin shims OR keep them as deprecated wrappers that call the launcher (do NOT delete if any are referenced by CI/docs — make them call-through shims). If they are NOT true duplicates (real behavioural differences), do NOT force-merge — document why and skip.
py_compile changed files; keep collection at baseline.
Return: files_renamed (count), importers_fixed (count), launcher_created (bool), scripts_consolidated (count), skipped_during_exec (list), collection_after.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Driver: git mv + precise importer fixes for SAFE-STATIC renames."""
import subprocess, sys, os, re, pathlib

ROOT = pathlib.Path("/home/polaris/wt/phase-file")
os.chdir(ROOT)

renamed = 0
importers_fixed = 0
fixed_files = set()

def sh(*args):
    r = subprocess.run(args, cwd=ROOT, capture_output=True, text=True)
    if r.returncode != 0:
        print("CMD FAIL:", args, file=sys.stderr)
        print(r.stdout, r.stderr, file=sys.stderr)
        raise SystemExit(2)
    return r.stdout

def gitmv(old, new):
    global renamed
    newp = ROOT / new
    newp.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    sh("git", "mv", old, new)
    renamed += 1
    print(f"MV {old} -> {new}")

def replace_in_file(relpath, replacements):
    """replacements: list of (old_substr, new_substr). Returns True if changed."""
    global importers_fixed
    p = ROOT / relpath
    txt = p.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    orig = txt
    for old, new in replacements:
        txt = txt.replace(old, new)
    if txt != orig:
        p.write_text(txt, encoding="utf-8")
        if relpath not in fixed_files:
            fixed_files.add(relpath)
            importers_fixed += 1
        print(f"  FIXED {relpath}")
        return True
    return False

# ---- src module renames with precise dotted-path replacements ----

# 1. honest_sweep_job_runner -> v30_sweep_job_runner
gitmv("src/polaris_graph/audit_ir/honest_sweep_job_runner.py",
      "src/polaris_graph/audit_ir/v30_sweep_job_runner.py")
for f in ["src/polaris_graph/audit_ir/__init__.py",
          "src/polaris_graph/audit_ir/inspector_router.py",
          "tests/polaris_graph/test_honest_sweep_job_runner.py"]:
    replace_in_file(f, [("audit_ir.honest_sweep_job_runner", "audit_ir.v30_sweep_job_runner"),
                        ("honest_sweep_job_runner.py", "v30_sweep_job_runner.py")])

# 2. pathB_capture -> benchmark_run_capture  (dotted: benchmark.pathB_capture)
gitmv("src/polaris_graph/benchmark/pathB_capture.py",
      "src/polaris_graph/benchmark/benchmark_run_capture.py")
# fix only files with real import of benchmark.pathB_capture
cap_importers = subprocess.run(
    ["grep", "-rlE", r"benchmark\.pathB_capture|benchmark import pathB_capture", "--include=*.py", "."],
    cwd=ROOT, capture_output=True, text=True).stdout.split()
for f in cap_importers:
    replace_in_file(f, [("benchmark.pathB_capture", "benchmark.benchmark_run_capture"),
                        ("benchmark import pathB_capture", "benchmark import benchmark_run_capture")])

# 3. pathB_runner -> benchmark_gate_runner
gitmv("src/polaris_graph/benchmark/pathB_runner.py",
      "src/polaris_graph/benchmark/benchmark_gate_runner.py")
run_importers = subprocess.run(
    ["grep", "-rlE", r"benchmark\.pathB_runner|benchmark import pathB_runner", "--include=*.py", "."],
    cwd=ROOT, capture_output=True, text=True).stdout.split()
for f in run_importers:
    replace_in_file(f, [("benchmark.pathB_runner", "benchmark.benchmark_gate_runner"),
                        ("benchmark import pathB_runner", "benchmark import benchmark_gate_runner")])
# by-path read_text in weekly_drift_report.py
replace_in_file("scripts/architecture/weekly_drift_report.py",
                [('"pathB_runner.py"', '"benchmark_gate_runner.py"')])

# 4. honest_pipeline -> provenance_verified_pipeline
gitmv("src/polaris_graph/honest_pipeline.py",
      "src/polaris_graph/provenance_verified_pipeline.py")
hp_importers = subprocess.run(
    ["grep", "-rlE", r"polaris_graph\.honest_pipeline|polaris_graph import honest_pipeline", "--include=*.py", "."],
    cwd=ROOT, capture_output=True, text=True).stdout.split()
for f in hp_importers:
    replace_in_file(f, [("polaris_graph.honest_pipeline", "polaris_graph.provenance_verified_pipeline"),
                        ("polaris_graph import honest_pipeline", "polaris_graph import provenance_verified_pipeline")])

# 5. honest_sweep_integration -> v30_sweep_integration
gitmv("src/polaris_graph/honest_sweep_integration.py",
      "src/polaris_graph/v30_sweep_integration.py")
hsi_importers = subprocess.run(
    ["grep", "-rlE", r"polaris_graph\.honest_sweep_integration|polaris_graph import honest_sweep_integration", "--include=*.py", "."],
    cwd=ROOT, capture_output=True, text=True).stdout.split()
for f in hsi_importers:
    replace_in_file(f, [("polaris_graph.honest_sweep_integration", "polaris_graph.v30_sweep_integration"),
                        ("polaris_graph import honest_sweep_integration", "polaris_graph import v30_sweep_integration")])

# 6. state_v3 -> state_lightweight
gitmv("src/polaris_graph/state_v3.py",
      "src/polaris_graph/state_lightweight.py")
sv3_importers = subprocess.run(
    ["grep", "-rlE", r"polaris_graph\.state_v3|polaris_graph import state_v3", "--include=*.py", "."],
    cwd=ROOT, capture_output=True, text=True).stdout.split()
for f in sv3_importers:
    replace_in_file(f, [("polaris_graph.state_v3", "polaris_graph.state_lightweight"),
                        ("polaris_graph import state_v3", "polaris_graph import state_lightweight")])

# 7. report_assembler_v2 -> grounded_bibliography_assembler
gitmv("src/polaris_graph/synthesis/report_assembler_v2.py",
      "src/polaris_graph/synthesis/grounded_bibliography_assembler.py")
ra_importers = subprocess.run(
    ["grep", "-rlE", r"synthesis\.report_assembler_v2|synthesis import report_assembler_v2", "--include=*.py", "."],
    cwd=ROOT, capture_output=True, text=True).stdout.split()
for f in ra_importers:
    replace_in_file(f, [("synthesis.report_assembler_v2", "synthesis.grounded_bibliography_assembler"),
                        ("synthesis import report_assembler_v2", "synthesis import grounded_bibliography_assembler")])

# 8. synthesizer_v2 -> section_synthesizer_parallel
gitmv("src/polaris_graph/synthesis/synthesizer_v2.py",
      "src/polaris_graph/synthesis/section_synthesizer_parallel.py")
sy_importers = subprocess.run(
    ["grep", "-rlE", r"synthesis\.synthesizer_v2|synthesis import synthesizer_v2", "--include=*.py", "."],
    cwd=ROOT, capture_output=True, text=True).stdout.split()
for f in sy_importers:
    replace_in_file(f, [("synthesis.synthesizer_v2", "synthesis.section_synthesizer_parallel"),
                        ("synthesis import synthesizer_v2", "synthesis import section_synthesizer_parallel")])

# 9. verifier_v2 -> verifier
gitmv("src/polaris_graph/synthesis/verifier_v2.py",
      "src/polaris_graph/synthesis/verifier.py")
vf_importers = subprocess.run(
    ["grep", "-rlE", r"synthesis\.verifier_v2|synthesis import verifier_v2", "--include=*.py", "."],
    cwd=ROOT, capture_output=True, text=True).stdout.split()
for f in vf_importers:
    replace_in_file(f, [("synthesis.verifier_v2", "synthesis.verifier"),
                        ("synthesis import verifier_v2", "synthesis import verifier")])

# 10. v30_contract_synthesizer -> contract_synthesizer
gitmv("src/polaris_graph/v30_contract_synthesizer.py",
      "src/polaris_graph/contract_synthesizer.py")
cs_importers = subprocess.run(
    ["grep", "-rlE", r"polaris_graph\.v30_contract_synthesizer|polaris_graph import v30_contract_synthesizer", "--include=*.py", "."],
    cwd=ROOT, capture_output=True, text=True).stdout.split()
for f in cs_importers:
    replace_in_file(f, [("polaris_graph.v30_contract_synthesizer", "polaris_graph.contract_synthesizer"),
                        ("polaris_graph import v30_contract_synthesizer", "polaris_graph import contract_synthesizer")])

# 11. lethal -> retrieval
gitmv("src/polaris_graph/wiki/mesh/retrieve/lethal.py",
      "src/polaris_graph/wiki/mesh/retrieve/retrieval.py")
# absolute imports
replace_in_file("scripts/_retired_2026_06_14/pg_mesh_preflight.py",
                [("retrieve.lethal import", "retrieve.retrieval import")])
replace_in_file("tests/integration/test_mesh_e2e.py",
                [("retrieve.lethal import", "retrieve.retrieval import")])
replace_in_file("tests/unit/test_mesh_lethal_retrieve.py",
                [("retrieve.lethal import", "retrieve.retrieval import")])
# relative imports
replace_in_file("src/polaris_graph/wiki/mesh/retrieve/__init__.py",
                [("from .lethal import", "from .retrieval import")])
replace_in_file("src/polaris_graph/wiki/mesh/qa/ask.py",
                [("from ..retrieve.lethal import", "from ..retrieve.retrieval import")])

print(f"\nRENAMED={renamed} IMPORTERS_FIXED={importers_fixed}")
